market_context: Nov 22 was a Saturday (no market). The 7 actual trading days were Nov 24-26, Nov 28 (Black Friday early close), and Dec 1-3. The period opened under the shadow of the Nov 19 FOMC minutes — revealed hawkish tone with 'many participants' favoring no more cuts in 2025, crashing December cut odds from ~70% to 42%. NVDA had beaten earnings same night (+3% afterhours) but digested poorly. By Nov 24-25, NY Fed Williams and SF Fed Daly both jawboned the Dec cut back onto the table, driving odds back to 81% by Nov 25. This triggered a powerful AI hyperscaler rally: AVGO +11%, GOOGL +6.3% (Willow quantum chip), AMD +5.5%. The pre-Thanksgiving holiday week saw all three major indexes post their best weeks since late June — S&P broke 6,800, Nasdaq sustained 23,000+. Nov 28 (Black Friday) saw thin volume in a holiday-shortened session. Dec 1 (Cyber Monday) was the first crack: Bitcoin -6% to sub-$86k crushed crypto-adjacent equities, with S&P -0.53% snapping a 5-day win streak. Dec 2-3 saw stabilization, with CRM reporting a blockbuster quarter afterhours Dec 3 — EPS $3.25 vs $2.86 est, Agentforce ARR +114% YoY to $1.4B and 9,500 paid deals. The period's regime was a rate-cut expectations recovery rally overlaid with selective AI monetization differentiation: old-style AI chip plays (ARM) under pressure from valuation while software AI monetization (CRM, PLTR) and safe-haven/value plays (GLD, DG) outperformed.

trades.thesis: AI hyperscaler with Willow quantum chip reveal imminent + Dec rate cut tailwind; cheap vs MSFT/META at ~22x forward
trades.catalyst: Google Willow quantum chip announcement Nov 25 + Fed officials (Williams/Daly) signaling Dec 25bp cut, odds recovering to 60%+
trades.lesson: Willow quantum chip was a 1-day pop; GOOGL gave back gains as AI momentum rotated to software (CRM) and the Dec rally was narrow. Entry timing was fine but position should have been taken on Nov 25 post-announcement dip instead of pre-announcement.

trades.thesis: AI custom ASIC structural beneficiary for hyperscalers; Dec earnings catalyst with AI chip demand accelerating
trades.catalyst: Fed dovish pivot (81% Dec cut odds) + AVGO custom AI ASIC TAM expansion; Nov 25 expected to surge with AI hyperscaler sentiment
trades.lesson: AVGO peaked at $411 around Dec 10-11, then crashed -12% on Dec 12 (likely profit-taking or sector rotation post-earnings on Dec 11). Entering Nov 24 was too early — should have waited for post-Dec-earnings-catalyst entry or used options to limit downside. The 20-day exit rule hurt here as AVGO was a winner mid-period.

trades.thesis: Gold structurally bid: Dec rate cut priced, real rates falling, CBs buying, geopolitical uncertainty; pullback from ATH = good entry
trades.catalyst: Dec 25bp cut 81% priced on Nov 25 + seasonal strength gold Dec-Jan + dollar softening on dovish Fed pivot
trades.lesson: Clean structural trade — gold benefited from Dec rate cut priced + real rates falling. The 8.4% gain in 20 days confirms gold as the right risk-reward in a dovish pivot + uncertainty environment. Should have sized larger given high-conviction macro backdrop.

trades.thesis: ARM trades at 80x+ forward P/E with royalty model capping upside; AI spending scrutiny mounting post-NVDA digest, FOMC hawkish overhang hits high-multiple names hardest
trades.catalyst: Nov FOMC minutes hawkish residue + AI valuation reset; no ARM-specific catalyst in near term, pure growth-decel short
trades.lesson: H1 lesson confirmed: short growth-decel + high-valuation SaaS/chip names in hawkish regime is high-conviction. ARM's royalty model = linear not exponential revenue growth despite AI narrative. The 15%+ short gain validates structural-short > catalyst-short thesis.

trades.thesis: LULU at multi-year lows (~17x forward), international acceleration (China +24%) offsets US weakness; Dec earnings beat likely as consensus too bearish
trades.catalyst: Q3 FY25 earnings Dec 11 + pre-Thanksgiving consumer discretionary rally + CEO succession + $1B buyback authorization
trades.lesson: Textbook contrarian long: consensus too bearish, valuation at floor, earnings beat + CEO change + buyback = triple catalyst. China +24% comp was the underappreciated upside surprise. Buying oversold quality at value is the right playbook in a recovering market.

trades.thesis: Dollar General at 52-week lows; Q3 earnings Dec 4 with high beat probability as value retail benefits from inflation-fatigued consumer; consensus reset too aggressively post-2024 guidance cut
trades.catalyst: Q3 FY25 earnings Dec 4 — EPS $1.28 vs ~$0.95 est (actual +35% beat); raised FY guidance + Black Friday consumer data
trades.lesson: Best trade of the period. The DG thesis was classic beaten-down earnings-recovery play: stock at lows, expectations demolished, set up for large positive surprise. The +44% EPS YoY beat drove +10% on Dec 4. Lesson: deep value + identifiable catalyst = highest-conviction trades.

trades.thesis: Bitcoin -6% on Dec 1 (worst day since March, below $86k) signals crypto deleveraging; COIN is high-beta crypto proxy with no fundamental floor if BTC corrects further
trades.catalyst: Bitcoin crashed to sub-$86k on Dec 1 breaking 5-day win streak; post-ATH profit-taking cycle began; COIN has 2x+ leverage to BTC downside
trades.lesson: Crypto beta short in risk-off environment works well. COIN moved from $260 to $226 as Bitcoin deleveraging continued through December. The signal (BTC worst day since March) was a clear regime shift indicator. High-beta shorts in down-trending underlying = clean risk/reward.

trades.thesis: PLTR structural defense AI beneficiary — AIP commercial deals + US NDAA defense budget; holding multi-year breakout, SaaS comps inapplicable (government contracts sticky)
trades.catalyst: Dec NDAA defense budget passage expected + ongoing AIP commercial deal closings; momentum regime intact post-S&P 500 inclusion
trades.lesson: Modest win — PLTR hit 193+ on Dec 19 before year-end selling to 177. The thesis was correct but the 20-day mechanical exit clipped the full move. PLTR peaked +14% from entry before reverting. Consider using catalysts as exit signals rather than pure calendar-based exits.

trades.thesis: CRM Q3 FY26 earnings afterhours Dec 3: Agentforce ARR growing explosively at 114% YoY; consensus underestimates AI monetization velocity; cRPO +11% signals durable growth
trades.catalyst: Q3 FY26 earnings Dec 3 afterhours: EPS $3.25 vs $2.86 est (14% beat); Agentforce 330% YoY ARR growth; 9,500 paid Agentforce deals; raised FY26 guidance to $41.45-41.55B
trades.lesson: Clean earnings-beat long. CRM peaked at $266 by Dec 26 before Jan 2 pullback to $253. Agentforce monetization was the right thesis — AI agents moving from demo to dollar. The Jan 2 exit was hit by holiday-shortened session liquidation. Full thesis intact but 20-day rule forced exit slightly below peak.

trades.thesis: META AI monetization through ad targeting accelerating; Llama ecosystem building moat; ~22x forward P/E reasonable for 20%+ EPS growth; rides pre-Thanksgiving mega-cap tech rally
trades.catalyst: Fed rate cut 80%+ priced for Dec + META AI-enhanced ad CPMs driving Q4 outperformance; mega-cap tech momentum
trades.lesson: Solid but not spectacular. META tracked broad market. The AI ad monetization thesis is correct but was already priced in to a degree. Lesson: META works as a market-beta long in risk-on environments but needs a specific underappreciated catalyst (e.g., Llama API monetization announcement) for outsized returns.

period_summary.regime_notes: The Nov 24 - Dec 3 period had two distinct regimes: (1) Nov 24-28 = FOMC-dovish recovery rally (80%+ Dec cut priced back in, AI hyperscalers surging, pre-holiday optimism); (2) Dec 1-3 = micro-cracks beginning (crypto deleveraging, 5-day win streak broken, selective rotation from AI chips to AI software). The key regime insight: hawkish FOMC minutes had already peaked in damage — the recovery trade (rate cut + value + software AI) was the right call. H1 lesson confirmed: structural short on high-multiple AI chip names (ARM) outperformed catalyst shorts. DG earnings recovery was the period's cleanest high-conviction trade: beaten-down value + identifiable catalyst + obliterated expectations = best PnL. AVGO long was the regime-read error — entered pre-earnings (correct) but Dec 12 massive selloff post-earnings peak was not anticipated; 20-day mechanical exit rule penalized what was an interim winner.
